archetype: Geologic Repository Nuclear Waste Automated Monitoring & Integrity System (GRONA-MIS)
domain: Deep geological nuclear waste disposal facility operations
description: A real-time, multi-sensor fusion system that monitors the physical, chemical, and radiological integrity of underground nuclear waste canisters within a sealed, multi-barrier repository. It is used by nuclear facility engineers and national regulator reviewers to detect deviations from the 10,000-year safety case, such as groundwater chemistry shifts, micro-seismic events, or container corrosion, triggering automated procedural responses and forensic data preservation.
rules: MUST comply with IAEA Safety Standards Series (SSS) and national nuclear regulator criteria (e.g., US 10 CFR 60, EU Directive 2011/70/Euratom) for data integrity, audit trails, and alarm logic.

MUST integrate heterogeneous sensor protocols: analog current loops (4-20mA) for corrosion probes, digital RS-485/MODBUS for seismic and pressure sensors, and custom radiation spectrometry (e.g., CANBERRA DIGITAL DMCA) over Ethernet/IP.
MUST enforce cryptographic data signing and immutable write-once storage for all sensor readings to satisfy legal admissibility for future generations and regulator evidence requirements.

MUST implement deterministic, hard real-time processing for seismic and gas release alarms with sub-second latency, running on an isolated safety-critical network segment air-gapped from corporate IT.

MUST model and compare sensor data against a validated, physics-based 'Base Line Evolution Model' that simulates repository conditions over millennia, using outputs from codes like TOUGH2 or PFLOTRAN.
anti_patterns: DO NOT assume cloud-native or always-on connectivity. The system must operate in a deep-underground, electromagnetically shielded environment with scheduled, secure data bursts to the surface; continuous high-bandwidth links are not permitted.

DO NOT treat sensors as plug-and-play. Every sensor deployment requires a rigorous calibration and qualification protocol documented in a 'Sensor Data Package' that is legally part of the facility's safety case.

DO NOT use generic time-series databases without chain-of-custody extensions. Data must be stored with 'provenance bundles' that link raw bytes to calibration certificates, technician IDs, and chain-of-custody timestamps in a tamper-evident ledger.
key_technical_challenges: Fusing low-SNR, asynchronous sensor data from thousands of points across heterogeneous, radiation-hardened fieldbuses into a single coherent repository state model while maintaining deterministic alarm timing.

Designing a 50+ year data architecture that remains semantically interpretable by future software/hardware generations, including format migration strategies and self-describing data packets with embedded metadata dictionaries.

Implementing a safety-critical, formally verified alarm logic engine that can combine inputs from physics models, live sensor feeds, and manual inspector logs without single points of failure, meeting SIL 3 (Safety Integrity Level) requirements.

recommended_stack.language: Rust (for sensor ingestion services & safety-critical logic) - Provides memory safety, zero-cost abstractions, and real-time control without a GC, critical for long-running underground processes.
recommended_stack.framework: Actix-web (for secure surface data ingestion API) - High-performance, actor-based model handles concurrent sensor data bursts efficiently with strong type safety.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (hypertables for high-frequency sensor data) + PostgreSQL with pgcrypto extensions. Chosen for its native time-series optimization, relational integrity for metadata, and robust cryptographic functions for data signing.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis (strictly for in-memory buffering of latest sensor states for the operator dashboard, configured as volatile ephemeral store with AOF disabled to prevent metadata persistence that could violate chain-of-custody).
recommended_stack.message_broker: Eclipse Mosquitto (MQTT broker) on a dedicated VLAN for local sensor network pub/sub. Used for lightweight, asynchronous telemetry distribution between underground nodes with QoS 2 for guaranteed delivery of critical alarms.
special_services: Custom 'Provenance Ledger' microservice: A private, permissioned blockchain-like append-only log (using PostgreSQL + cryptographic hash chains) to immutably record every data transformation, calibration event, or manual override.

Physics Model Adapter Service: A FaaS-like container that runs licensed simulation codes (TOUGH2) on a scheduled basis, ingesting their output to update the baseline model and generate deviation thresholds.

compliance_flags: IAEA Safety Standards - SSG-1 (Fundamentals of Safety)
IAEA SSG-14 (Safety of Radioactive Waste Management)
National Nuclear Regulator License Condition LC 20 (Records)
ISO 19450:2015 (Nuclear energy - Quality assurance for safety)
10 CFR 50, Appendix B (Quality Assurance Criteria for Nuclear Power Plants) - applied by analogy to waste facilities
